methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunday Robotics is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Xiongyi Cui and Linxi Fan. The comparable universe for this sector comprises 9 identified peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the narrow filter criteria—same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and development stages.

Without a sufficiently dense peer set at the seed stage, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be credibly applied to Sunday Robotics. A complete valuation would require alternative approaches: discounted cash flow analysis (if forward financials exist), venture capital method benchmarking against sector-typical ownership and return targets, or structured expert judgment from investors with direct experience in early-stage robotics. The key uncertainty remains the absence of narrow-filter comparables; future revaluation becomes viable once the company reaches a later stage or as additional seed-stage robotics peers emerge with published metrics.
